commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Protected maintainer-labeled draft plus marketplace trust/default decisions require owner review; no narrow automated repair is identified.; Cleared: No concrete supply-chain or secret-handling defect was found in the diff; the hosted marketplace trust boundary remains a maintainer merge-risk decision. (sha=cdb7dda1efff5f8bdf4e88c056ff69b6a6053218)
commands.review_summary:
Adds hosted catalog feed/source profile types and defaults, sourceRef validation/filtering, profile-authorized guarded feed hosts, and ClawHub feed candidate install resolution in the official external plugin catalog code and tests.

PR surface: Source +296, Tests +429. Total +725 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a hosted marketplace feature slice, not a bug report. The branch behavior is source-checkable, and the PR body plus a live feed check show the current ClawHub feed still uses public-clawhub candidates.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Protected maintainer-labeled draft plus marketplace trust/default decisions require owner review; no narrow automated repair is identified.

target.title: Add hosted catalog source profile validation
target.body:
<!-- hosted-feed-stack:start -->
## Hosted Marketplace Feed Stack

This is PR5 in the hosted marketplace feed stack. The stack should land in order, with maintainer review at each step before the later PRs wire hosted marketplace data into broader user flows.

1. #95846: refactor the bundled external plugin catalog toward the feed-shaped data model.
2. #95868: add the lazy hosted marketplace feed loader for the configured public feed URL, with guarded HTTPS fetch, checksum, schema, size, timeout, and bundled fallback behavior.
3. #95877: add accepted hosted snapshot fallback so a failed or unchanged hosted fetch can reuse the last valid feed payload.
4. #95964: persist hosted marketplace feed snapshots in OpenClaw state.
5. #95969: add marketplace source profile validation for hosted feed install candidates.
6. #95981: add configurable `marketplaces.feeds` and `marketplaces.sources` profiles.
7. #96155: add `openclaw plugins marketplace refresh` as the explicit manual snapshot refresh path.
8. #96158: add `openclaw plugins marketplace entries` as the explicit manual read path over hosted, snapshot, or bundled fallback entries.
9. #96194: add marketplace feed telemetry.

The broader follow-up after this stack is to decide whether and how marketplace entries should feed install/search/startup behavior, add automatic conditional refresh using `ETag` / `Last-Modified`, or move to ClawHub producer-side validation for `/v1/feeds/plugins` and `/v1/feeds/skills`.
<!-- hosted-feed-stack:end -->

## Stack Context

This is step 5 in the hosted feed rollout, stacked on `feeds-hosted-catalog-persistent-snapshot` after:

- #95846 reshapes the bundled external plugin catalog as a feed-shaped fallback.
- #95868 adds the guarded hosted feed loader.
- #95877 adds the last-known-good snapshot fallback contract.
- #95964 persists hosted catalog snapshots in OpenClaw state.

## RFC Alignment

This PR adds the first local feed/source profile scaffolding from RFC #19. The important boundary is that profile names are local OpenClaw configuration. A hosted feed can refer to a configured `sourceRef`, but it cannot define registry domains, credentials, or trust roots for the client.

This matches the live ClawHub plugin feed at `https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins`, which uses `public-clawhub` candidates. The PR also keeps the older register host allowed for transition, while `registry.openclaw.ai` is still being brought online.

## Summary

- Adds local profile types for hosted catalog feeds and package sources.
- Adds the default `clawhub-public` feed profile pointing at `https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins`.
- Adds the default `public-clawhub` source profile for ClawHub candidates and keeps `public-npm` for npm-backed candidates.
- Lets named local feed profiles authorize their configured HTTPS host through the existing SSRF guard path.
- Keeps direct ad hoc `feedUrl` overrides constrained to the public host allowlist, including `clawhub.ai` and the earlier register host.
- Filters feed entries that opt into `install.candidates[].sourceRef` when the source ref is missing or not configured locally.
- Applies the same source-ref filtering to hosted responses and hosted snapshot fallback.
- Recognizes RFC-style `id` and `title` fields for catalog identity and labels so live ClawHub entries are not dropped before the later install-mapping slice.

## Deliberately Not Included

- Feed/source profile persistence in user config files.
- Secret-reference resolution or authenticated feed/source access.
- Signed feed envelopes or trust-key configuration.
- Scheduled refresh, `maxStale`, or stale-catalog status reporting.
- Changing plugin install execution to consume candidate source profiles or translating RFC candidates into installer specs.
- Search, install UX, skills, or telemetry.

## Validation

- `pnpm exec oxfmt src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint --deny-warnings src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `CI=1 timeout 180s node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
This PR validates hosted marketplace install source refs against local source profiles and accepts the live ClawHub plugin feed format now served as `schemaVersion: 2`.

Real environment tested:
WSL Ubuntu 24.04, `/root/src/openclaw-feeds-hosted-catalog-prep`, PR5 and cumulative hosted marketplace feed stack through PR8 at `13cea8e00e7`, built source tree with `pnpm build`, isolated OpenClaw state via `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof.rKXnUX`, and the live ClawHub feed endpoint `https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

1. On PR5: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts --reporter=dot`
2. On PR8 after rebasing PR6-PR8: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-refresh.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-entries.test.ts src/plugins/marketplace.test.ts src/config/zod-schema.marketplaces.test.ts --reporter=dot`
3. `curl -sS -D - https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins -o /tmp/clawhub-plugins.json`
4. `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof.rKXnUX node openclaw.mjs plugins marketplace entries --feed-url https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins`

Evidence after fix:
PR5 focused catalog tests passed: `1 passed / 38 tests`. Full stack focused tests passed across 3 Vitest shards: `1 passed / 8 tests`, `2 passed / 7 tests`, and `2 passed / 70 tests`. Live curl showed `HTTP/1.1 200 OK`, `X-Content-Sha256: 99b2608a539fe54d0d0ded2182574b62b8b772bd2011d9eb497147be23ff2c2e`, `Last-Modified: Wed, 24 Jun 2026 13:06:20 GMT`, and a body beginning with `{ "schemaVersion": 2, "id": "clawhub-official" ... }`. The built CLI returned `Source: hosted`, `Entries: 59`, `Feed: clawhub-official sequence 9`, and live ClawHub plugin entries.

Observed result after fix:
The live ClawHub v2 feed is accepted, entries with `public-clawhub` source refs resolve to `clawhub:` and npm install choices, and unsupported future schema versions still stay out of legacy parsing in tests.

What was not tested:
No live install was run. This PR validates and lists feed metadata only; install wiring remains a later slice.
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
